project: MCP-Unique Org-Scale Context Retrieval Benchmark Extension
description: Extend CodeContextBench with an 'MCP-unique / org-scale context retrieval' layer measuring what local-only agents cannot do: cross-repo + cross-org discovery, symbol-resolution, dependency tracing, and deep-search-driven investigation in polyrepo environments. Key differentiators vs existing cross-repo tasks: org-scale quantity (5-20 repos per task), structured closed-world oracle evaluation, customer-framed prompts from GTM use cases, and Deep Search-specific task variants.
nonGoals: Do not rebuild the existing benchmark framework — extend it
Do not require granular human-subject timing studies
Do not assume the baseline agent can see all repos locally
Do not change existing task scoring or verifier logic
Do not modify Harbor agent code (claude_baseline_agent.py)
Cross-host (GitHub + GitLab) tasks are deferred — measure cross-org for now

designDecisions.Q1_fixtures: Create sg-benchmarks mirrors for 7 missing repos (client-go, api, express, lodash, prisma, loki, mimir)
designDecisions.Q2_differentiators: Focus on org-scale quantity (5-20 repos), structured oracle evaluation, and customer-framed prompts. Cross-host is less important than these.
designDecisions.Q3_crosshost: Skip cross-host for now. G-family tasks become cross-org (repos from different GitHub orgs, not different hosts). Revisit cross-host when we have a multi-host SG instance.
designDecisions.Q4_oracle: Closed-world exhaustive oracles. Agent-curated via automated SG queries during task setup — no human involvement. May not be perfect but gives best precision measurement possible.
designDecisions.Q5_suites: Per-category suites: ccb_mcp_crossrepo_tracing, ccb_mcp_security, ccb_mcp_migration, ccb_mcp_incident, ccb_mcp_onboarding, ccb_mcp_compliance, ccb_mcp_crosshost (TBD), ccb_mcp_domain, ccb_mcp_org, ccb_mcp_platform
designDecisions.Q6_category_i: Category I uses hybrid: test_ratio (does generated code compile/pass tests) + context verification (did agent find the right API signatures). Both eval dimensions scored.
designDecisions.Q7_deepsearch: Design Deep Search-specific tasks as options within suites, especially for onboarding and exploratory codebase Q&A.
designDecisions.Q8_thresholds: No score thresholds initially. Run starter pack, collect actual scores, then calibrate thresholds based on agent performance.
designDecisions.Q9_selection: Create configs/selected_mcp_unique_tasks.json + update run_selected_tasks.sh to accept --selection-file
designDecisions.Q10_retrieval: Retrieval metrics must work for both baseline and MCP configs. Count oracle items found via any tool (local or MCP) — the metric is oracle coverage regardless of method.

F.description: Compliance / audit / provenance in regulated environments

G.description: Cross-org discovery (repos from different orgs). Cross-host TBD.

I.description: Agentic coding correctness using org-wide context

taskFamilies.description: Trace dependencies, call chains, and blast radius across repos
Config keys, feature flags, event names, symbol resolution across repos
CVE mapping, lockfile scanning, secret detection, auth audit
Framework upgrades, API deprecation, schema migration across repos
Error tracing, timeout debugging, circuit breaker audit across services
Architecture maps, API consumption, end-to-end flow understanding
PII tracing, audit log validation, evidence bundles for controls
Cross-org discovery (different GitHub orgs, shared interfaces, duplicates)
Domain-specific tracing: risk factors, pricing, trade IDs, consistency checks
Generate code using correct cross-repo API signatures and patterns
Service templates, ownership discovery, deprecated endpoint cleanup

userStories.title: Use Case Registry JSON schema
userStories.description: Define the JSON schema for the use case registry encoding each of the 100 use cases with metadata, MCP capability requirements, oracle types, and fixture references.
acceptanceCriteria: schemas/use_case_registry.schema.json exists and is valid JSON Schema (draft 2020-12 or 07)

Schema defines required fields: use_case_id (integer 1-100), category (enum A-J), title (string), customer_prompt (string), mcp_suite (enum of 10 ccb_mcp_* suites from suiteMapping), task_family (string)

Schema defines mcp_unique (boolean), mcp_capabilities_required (array of enum: keyword_search, nls_search, find_references, go_to_definition, read_file, deepsearch, compare_revisions, diff_search, commit_search)

Schema defines oracle_type (enum: deterministic_json, exit_code, unit_tests, rubric_judge, hybrid), oracle_check_types (array of enum: file_set_match, symbol_resolution, dependency_chain, provenance, keyword_presence, json_schema_match, test_ratio)
Schema defines repo_set_id (string ref), difficulty (enum: easy, medium, hard), estimated_repos_needed (integer)
Schema defines deepsearch_relevant (boolean) — marks tasks designed to benefit from Deep Search
Schema defines optional salesforce_flags and gong_evidence objects
python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('schemas/use_case_registry.schema.json'))" succeeds
userStories.notes: Key change from v1: mcp_suite field maps to the 10 ccb_mcp_* suites (not the 8 SDLC suites). Added deepsearch_relevant flag and test_ratio to oracle_check_types for Category I tasks.

userStories.title: Repo-set fixture schema and format
userStories.description: Define the JSON schema and directory structure for repo-set fixtures specifying which repos are local vs MCP-only. Focus on cross-org (different GitHub orgs) rather than cross-host.
acceptanceCriteria: schemas/repo_set_fixture.schema.json exists and is valid JSON Schema
Schema defines: repo_set_id (string), description (string), repos (array of repo objects)
Each repo object has: host (string, default github.com), org (string), repo_name (string), full_name (string = org/repo_name), revision (string, git ref), logical_name (string)
Each repo object has: access_mode (enum: local_checkout, mcp_only, both), sourcegraph_indexed (boolean), sourcegraph_mirror (optional string, sg-benchmarks mirror name if public repo not indexed)
Schema defines local_checkout_repos (array of full_name strings), mcp_only_repos (array of full_name strings)
Schema defines cross_org (boolean) — true when repos span multiple GitHub orgs
Schema defines language_mix (array of strings), primary_language (string)
fixtures/ directory exists with fixtures/repo_sets/ subdirectory and README.md
python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('schemas/repo_set_fixture.schema.json'))" succeeds
userStories.notes: Simplified from v1: host is just a string (not an enum of hosts) since we're deferring cross-host. cross_org boolean replaces the old multi-host requirement. The sourcegraph_mirror field is critical for repos that aren't natively indexed — it points to the sg-benchmarks mirror name.

userStories.title: Create sg-benchmarks mirrors for missing repos
userStories.description: Create Sourcegraph-indexed mirrors for the 7 repos needed by fixtures that are not natively indexed: kubernetes/client-go, kubernetes/api, expressjs/express, lodash/lodash, prisma/prisma, grafana/loki, grafana/mimir.
acceptanceCriteria: sg-benchmarks/kubernetes-client-go mirror exists on GitHub with HEAD pinned to a recent stable tag
sg-benchmarks/kubernetes-api mirror exists on GitHub with HEAD pinned to a recent stable tag
sg-benchmarks/expressjs-express mirror exists on GitHub with HEAD pinned to a recent stable tag
sg-benchmarks/lodash mirror exists on GitHub with HEAD pinned to a recent stable tag
sg-benchmarks/prisma-prisma mirror exists on GitHub with HEAD pinned to a recent stable tag
sg-benchmarks/grafana-loki mirror exists on GitHub with HEAD pinned to a recent stable tag
sg-benchmarks/grafana-mimir mirror exists on GitHub with HEAD pinned to a recent stable tag
All 7 mirrors are indexed in Sourcegraph (verify with mcp__sourcegraph__keyword_search on each)
Each mirror uses the orphan-commit approach for repos >2GB (shallow clone at tag, checkout --orphan, commit, push)
Revision SHAs recorded in a tracking file: configs/sg_mirror_revisions.json
userStories.notes: Follow the proven sg-benchmarks mirror pattern from LoCoBench/LargeRepo tasks. Use orphan-commit approach for large repos (grafana/loki is ~500MB). Pin to recent stable releases, not HEAD. Record the exact revision so fixtures can reference it. SG indexing may take hours after push — verify before marking done.

userStories.title: Seed use case registry from 100 use cases
userStories.description: Create the use case registry JSON populated from docs/refined-mcp-use-cases-and-benchmarks.md. All 100 entries with full metadata for priority categories (A, B, D, E), stubs for the rest.
acceptanceCriteria: configs/use_case_registry.json exists with all 100 use case entries
Each entry has: use_case_id, category, title, customer_prompt (from refined doc), mcp_suite (from suiteMapping), task_family
Categories A (1-10), B (11-20), D (31-40), E (41-50) have fully populated: mcp_unique, mcp_capabilities_required, oracle_type, difficulty, estimated_repos_needed, deepsearch_relevant
Categories C, F, G, H, I, J have title + customer_prompt + category + mcp_suite but oracle_type='tbd'
mcp_suite uses the new per-category suite names: ccb_mcp_crossrepo_tracing for A, ccb_mcp_security for B, etc.
salesforce_flags populated based on refined doc evidence anchors
deepsearch_relevant=true for E41-E50 (onboarding) and selected J tasks (exploratory Q&A)
configs/use_case_registry.json validates against schemas/use_case_registry.schema.json
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('configs/use_case_registry.json')); assert len(d['use_cases'])==100" succeeds
userStories.notes: G-category tasks should have mcp_suite='ccb_mcp_crossorg' (not crosshost). Mark G-category entries as deferred with a note that cross-host is TBD. Category I tasks should have oracle_type='hybrid' (test_ratio + context verification).

userStories.title: TaskSpec schema merging SWE-Factory + PRDBench patterns
userStories.description: Define the unified TaskSpec schema for MCP-unique benchmark instances. Merges SWE-Factory exit-code-first grading with PRDBench PRD-centered criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: schemas/mcp_task_spec.schema.json exists and is valid JSON Schema
Schema defines: id (pattern CCX-<family>-<NNN>), family, use_case_id, category (A-J), mcp_suite (ccb_mcp_* name)
Schema defines prd section: user_story, constraints (array), success_definition, seed_prompt

Schema defines artifacts.oracle: required_files array, required_symbols array, required_references array, dependency_chains array. NO min_recall/min_precision fields (thresholds deferred for calibration)
Schema defines evaluation section: modes, checks (array of {type, params}), eval_script, pass_exit_code
Check types include test_ratio for Category I: params {test_command, context_check_command}
Schema defines logging.required_metrics array
Schema defines optional rubric_judge section and optional deepsearch_variant section
python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('schemas/mcp_task_spec.schema.json'))" succeeds
userStories.notes: Key change from v1: removed min_recall/min_precision from oracle (thresholds deferred per Q8). Added test_ratio check type for Category I. Added deepsearch_variant section for tasks that have a DS-specific variant.

userStories.title: Oracle and evaluation check library
userStories.description: Implement reusable Python library of deterministic oracle checks that eval.sh scripts invoke. No thresholds hardcoded — returns raw scores for calibration.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/oracle_checks.py exists with check functions
check_file_set_match(answer_files, oracle_files) -> dict returns {recall, precision, f1, missing, extra, matched} — NO pass/fail, just raw scores
check_symbol_resolution(answer_symbols, oracle_symbols) -> dict returns {matched, missing, extra, recall, precision}
check_dependency_chain(answer_chain, oracle_chain) -> dict returns {matched_steps, missing_steps, order_correct, chain_recall}
check_provenance(answer, must_cite_paths, must_cite_repos) -> dict returns {citations_found, citations_valid, provenance_score}
check_keyword_presence(answer_text, required_keywords) -> dict returns {found, missing, total, keyword_recall}
check_json_schema(answer_json, schema_path) -> dict returns {valid, errors}
check_test_ratio(test_command, workspace_dir) -> dict returns {passed, failed, total, ratio} — for Category I code-gen tasks
run_all_checks(answer_path, task_spec_path) -> dict aggregates all check results + computes composite_score as mean of individual check scores
Composite score written to stdout as single float. Exit 0 if composite > 0, exit 1 if composite == 0 (total failure)
Standalone CLI mode: python3 oracle_checks.py --answer <path> --spec <path>
Stdlib only. python3 -m py_compile succeeds. Doctests pass.
userStories.notes: Key change from v1: NO pass/fail thresholds. Returns raw recall/precision/F1 scores. The composite score is a simple mean across all configured checks. Exit code 0 means 'agent did something' (composite > 0), exit 1 means 'total failure' (composite == 0). This enables threshold calibration later. Added check_test_ratio for Category I.

userStories.title: Automated validity filtering (fail2pass gate)
userStories.description: Implement validity gate ensuring every task is non-degenerate: gold oracle output scores > 0, empty output scores 0.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/validate_mcp_task_instance.py exists with CLI: --task-dir, --verbose, --fix
Gold answer check: runs oracle_checks against tests/oracle_answer.json — composite score MUST be > 0
Empty answer check: runs oracle_checks against empty answer — composite score MUST be 0
Reports: VALID, DEGENERATE_PASS, DEGENERATE_FAIL, BROKEN per task
Summary + exit code 0/1
--fix mode generates stub oracle_answer.json from task_spec.json
python3 -m py_compile succeeds
userStories.notes: Same as v1 but adapted for the threshold-free scoring approach. Gold answer must score > 0, empty must score exactly 0.

userStories.title: Agent-based oracle curation tool
userStories.description: Build a tool that uses Sourcegraph MCP to automatically generate exhaustive closed-world oracle_answer.json files for MCP-unique tasks. No human curation needed.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/curate_oracle.py exists with CLI: --task-dir DIR, --task-spec PATH, --verify, --verbose
Given a task_spec.json with seed_prompt and repo_set_id, uses Sourcegraph MCP tools (keyword_search, find_references, go_to_definition, read_file) to discover all matching files/symbols/chains
For file_set_match oracles: searches all repos in the fixture for files matching the task's search criteria, builds exhaustive list
For symbol_resolution oracles: uses find_references and go_to_definition to trace all definitions and usages, builds complete symbol map
For dependency_chain oracles: traces import/call chains across repos, builds ordered chain
Writes oracle_answer.json with all discovered items + metadata (discovery_method, timestamp, repos_searched)
Writes oracle_curation_log.json with full trace of SG queries made, results returned, and filtering decisions
--verify mode re-runs the oracle through eval.sh to confirm it passes the validity gate
Handles SG rate limits and pagination gracefully
Can be run incrementally: if oracle_answer.json exists, merges new findings
python3 -m py_compile scripts/curate_oracle.py succeeds
userStories.notes: This is the key automation that makes closed-world oracles feasible without human involvement. The tool should be thorough: for a file_set_match oracle, it should search EVERY repo in the fixture, not just the ones it expects to find results in. The curation log provides auditability. May not find 100% of items but should get close for well-scoped tasks. Run it, review the log, re-run if gaps are spotted.

userStories.title: Repo-set fixtures for starter pack
userStories.description: Create repo-set fixtures using real repos (natively indexed or sg-benchmarks mirrors). Focus on cross-org, not cross-host.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 repo-set fixtures in fixtures/repo_sets/

fixtures/repo_sets/kubernetes-ecosystem.json: kubernetes/kubernetes (indexed) + sg-benchmarks/kubernetes-client-go (mirror) + sg-benchmarks/kubernetes-api (mirror) + etcd-io/etcd (indexed). local: kubernetes/kubernetes only, mcp_only: rest. cross_org=true

fixtures/repo_sets/nodejs-web-stack.json: nodejs/node (indexed) + sg-benchmarks/expressjs-express (mirror) + sg-benchmarks/lodash (mirror) + sg-benchmarks/prisma-prisma (mirror). local: nodejs/node, mcp_only: rest. cross_org=true

fixtures/repo_sets/python-ml-stack.json: scikit-learn (indexed) + numpy (indexed) + pandas (indexed) + scipy (indexed). local: scikit-learn, mcp_only: rest. All natively indexed, no mirrors needed. cross_org=true

fixtures/repo_sets/grafana-observability.json: grafana/grafana (indexed) + sg-benchmarks/grafana-loki (mirror) + sg-benchmarks/grafana-mimir (mirror). local: grafana/grafana, mcp_only: rest. cross_org=false (same org)

fixtures/repo_sets/multi-org-go.json: kubernetes/kubernetes + etcd-io/etcd + grafana/grafana (all indexed, different orgs). local: one repo, mcp_only: rest. cross_org=true. Replaces the old cross-host-go fixture
Each fixture pinned to specific revision SHAs (from configs/sg_mirror_revisions.json for mirrors)
All repos verified as searchable in Sourcegraph
Each fixture validates against schemas/repo_set_fixture.schema.json
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (mirrors created). The python-ml-stack is the easiest fixture since all 4 repos are natively indexed. Use it for early testing. The multi-org-go fixture replaces the old cross-host fixture — it exercises cross-org discovery (k8s org + etcd org + grafana org) without needing a separate code host.

userStories.title: Task generation templates
userStories.description: Create templates for generating MCP-unique task files. Templates map to the new ccb_mcp_* suite structure.
acceptanceCriteria: templates/mcp_unique_task/ directory exists with template files
task.toml.j2: valid task.toml with mcp_suite, use_case_id, repo_set_id, mcp_unique=true in task metadata
instruction.md.j2: customer-framed prompt, explicit artifacts/answer.json output requirement, no oracle leakage
eval.sh.j2: exit-code-first evaluator calling oracle_checks.py, writes reward.txt
task_spec.json.j2: full TaskSpec from registry + fixture
Dockerfile.j2: baseline (clones local_checkout_repos only)
Dockerfile.sg_only.j2: sg_only variant (no clones or truncated clones)
Templates use Python string.Template (no Jinja2 dep)
README.md documents all template variables
All templates produce valid output when filled with example data
userStories.notes: Suite directory is benchmarks/<mcp_suite>/<task_slug>/. Template eval.sh must handle sg_only mode guard and reward.txt path correctly.

userStories.title: Task generator CLI
userStories.description: CLI script that reads registry + fixtures, fills templates, writes task directories, and optionally runs oracle curation.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/generate_mcp_unique_tasks.py with argparse CLI
--use-case-ids, --category, --family, --all, --out, --dry-run flags
Resolves mcp_suite to target benchmarks/<ccb_mcp_*>/ directory, creates task slug
Resolves repo_set_id to fixture, populates Dockerfile with local_checkout_repos clone commands
--curate-oracle flag: after generating task files, runs scripts/curate_oracle.py to auto-populate oracle_answer.json
Generated tasks validate syntactically (py_compile, bash -n, json.load)
Skips oracle_type='tbd' entries unless --include-stubs
Creates benchmarks/<suite>/ directory if it doesn't exist
python3 scripts/generate_mcp_unique_tasks.py --help and --dry-run succeed
userStories.notes: The --curate-oracle flag is the main workflow: generate task skeleton, then auto-populate oracle. This is the zero-human-involvement path for task authoring.

userStories.title: Starter tasks — Category A: Cross-repo dependency tracing (3 tasks)
userStories.description: Author 3 complete tasks for ccb_mcp_crossrepo_tracing. First end-to-end validated tasks, reference implementation for all future MCP-unique tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Task CCX-dep-trace-001 (A1 — blast radius): kubernetes-ecosystem fixture. Oracle: repos importing a shared pkg. eval.sh: file_set_match
Task CCX-dep-trace-004 (A4 — API call chain): grafana-observability fixture. Oracle: ordered call chain across repos. eval.sh: dependency_chain + provenance
Task CCX-dep-trace-010 (A10 — symbol resolution): kubernetes-ecosystem fixture. Oracle: exact repo+file+symbol. eval.sh: symbol_resolution
Each task: task.toml, instruction.md (customer-framed), task_spec.json, Dockerfile, Dockerfile.sg_only, eval.sh, oracle_answer.json (agent-curated), oracle_checks.py
All 3 pass validity gate. Oracle curated via curate_oracle.py
Tasks in benchmarks/ccb_mcp_crossrepo_tracing/<task_slug>/
Registered in configs/selected_mcp_unique_tasks.json
userStories.notes: These are the reference tasks. Oracle auto-curated then manually spot-checked for completeness. instruction.md does NOT leak oracle file/symbol names.

userStories.title: Starter tasks — Category B: Vulnerability remediation (2 tasks)
userStories.description: Author 2 tasks for ccb_mcp_security.
acceptanceCriteria: Task CCX-vuln-remed-011 (B11 — CVE remediation): nodejs-web-stack fixture. Oracle: {repo, file, version} tuples. eval.sh: file_set_match + keyword_presence
Task CCX-vuln-remed-014 (B14 — missing auth middleware): grafana-observability fixture. Oracle: {repo, file, endpoint} tuples. eval.sh: file_set_match + provenance
Both pass validity gate. Oracle agent-curated
Tasks in benchmarks/ccb_mcp_security/
Registered in selection file
userStories.notes: B-family = highest revenue ICP pain point ($6.8M security remediation). Use real dependency files from real repos.

userStories.title: Starter tasks — Category D: Incident debugging (1 task)
userStories.description: Author 1 task for ccb_mcp_incident.
acceptanceCriteria: Task CCX-incident-031 (D31 — error to code path): multi-org-go fixture. Oracle: {repo, file, function} tuples. eval.sh: file_set_match + keyword_presence
Oracle includes decoy files (similar but different error strings) to test precision
Passes validity gate. Oracle agent-curated
Task in benchmarks/ccb_mcp_incident/
Registered in selection file
userStories.notes: Error string should be specific enough for bounded oracle but generic enough for false positives (decoys). Tests agent's ability to filter noise.

userStories.title: Starter tasks — Category E: Onboarding comprehension (2 tasks, incl. 1 Deep Search variant)
userStories.description: Author 2 tasks for ccb_mcp_onboarding. One is a standard retrieval task, one is designed specifically for Deep Search.
acceptanceCriteria: Task CCX-onboard-041 (E41 — API consumption): python-ml-stack fixture. Oracle: {repo, file, call_site} tuples. eval.sh: file_set_match + provenance

Task CCX-onboard-050-ds (E50 — end-to-end flow, Deep Search variant): kubernetes-ecosystem fixture. Oracle: ordered flow steps + narrative quality. eval.sh: dependency_chain + provenance. rubric_judge enabled for narrative quality
CCX-onboard-050-ds instruction explicitly frames as an exploratory question: 'Explain how X works end-to-end across these repos' — designed to benefit from Deep Search's synthesis capability
CCX-onboard-050-ds has tests/criteria.json with PRDBench AAA criteria for narrative quality
Both pass validity gate (deterministic checks only — rubric judge is supplementary)
Tasks in benchmarks/ccb_mcp_onboarding/
Registered in selection file with deepsearch_relevant=true for CCX-onboard-050-ds
userStories.notes: The DS variant (CCX-onboard-050-ds) is the first task specifically designed for Deep Search. The instruction should be open-ended enough that DS synthesis provides real value over keyword_search + read_file. Hybrid eval: deterministic checks verify file/symbol coverage, rubric judge evaluates explanation quality.

userStories.title: Starter tasks — Category G: Cross-org discovery (2 tasks)
userStories.description: Author 2 tasks for ccb_mcp_crossorg. These exercise discovery across repos from different GitHub organizations.
acceptanceCriteria: Task CCX-crossorg-061 (G61 — cross-org interface impl): multi-org-go fixture. Oracle: {org, repo, file, symbol} tuples from 2+ orgs. eval.sh: symbol_resolution with unique_orgs_touched >= 2
Task CCX-crossorg-066 (G66 — authoritative repo): multi-org-go fixture. Oracle: which repo is source of truth + evidence. eval.sh: keyword_presence + provenance
Both require discovering items across repos from different orgs (kubernetes org + etcd org + grafana org)
Oracle agent-curated. Both pass validity gate
Tasks in benchmarks/ccb_mcp_crossorg/
Registered in selection file
userStories.notes: Cross-org replaces cross-host for now. The multi-org-go fixture has repos from 3 different GitHub orgs, so the agent must search broadly. Baseline with 1 local repo has limited oracle coverage.

userStories.title: Deep Search exploration tasks (2 additional tasks)
userStories.description: Author 2 additional tasks specifically designed for Deep Search: one for exploratory codebase Q&A, one for architectural synthesis. Place in relevant suites.
acceptanceCriteria: Task CCX-explore-042-ds (E42 — architecture map, DS variant): python-ml-stack fixture. 'Map the data flow from raw array creation through scientific computation across these repos.' eval.sh: dependency_chain + provenance. rubric_judge for synthesis quality

Task CCX-explore-091-ds (J91 — service template discovery, DS variant): kubernetes-ecosystem fixture. 'Find the canonical patterns for deploying new services and how they're documented across repos.' eval.sh: file_set_match + keyword_presence. rubric_judge for comprehensiveness
Both have tests/criteria.json with AAA criteria emphasizing synthesis, not just retrieval
Both marked deepsearch_relevant=true in selection file
Instruction framing is exploratory/open-ended — designed so DS synthesis adds value beyond keyword search
Both pass validity gate on deterministic checks
Tasks placed in ccb_mcp_onboarding and ccb_mcp_platform respectively
userStories.notes: These are the Deep Search showcase tasks. The key design principle: frame the question so that synthesizing across many files provides better answers than reading individual files. DS should excel at 'explain the pattern across all repos' questions.

userStories.title: MCP retrieval metrics extractor (baseline + MCP compatible)
userStories.description: Extract context retrieval KPIs from agent logs. Must work for both baseline (local tool usage) and MCP configs.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ccb_metrics/retrieval.py exists

extract_retrieval_metrics(task_dir, oracle_items) -> dict with: oracle_coverage, time_to_first_oracle_hit_ms, unique_repos_touched, unique_orgs_touched, tool_call_counts, mcp_tool_counts, local_tool_counts
Oracle items = {repo, path, symbol?} tuples from task_spec.json
For MCP: parses mcp__ prefixed tool calls from transcript
For baseline: parses Grep, Read, Glob tool calls — extracts file paths and matches against oracle items
oracle_coverage counts items found via ANY tool (local or MCP) — fair comparison
Standalone CLI + writes retrieval_metrics.json
Handles missing transcripts gracefully
python3 -m py_compile succeeds
userStories.notes: Key decision (Q10): oracle coverage counts items found via any method. This means baseline CAN score non-zero if it finds oracle items in local repos. The comparison is fair: both configs measured by same metric. MCP advantage shows up in coverage of mcp_only repos (baseline can't access them).

userStories.title: Selection file and config wiring
userStories.description: Create configs/selected_mcp_unique_tasks.json and update run_selected_tasks.sh to accept --selection-file.
acceptanceCriteria: configs/selected_mcp_unique_tasks.json exists with all starter pack tasks (12 tasks)
Each entry has: task_id, mcp_suite (ccb_mcp_*), use_case_category, language, difficulty, repo, mcp_benefit_score, task_dir, use_case_id, deepsearch_relevant
configs/run_selected_tasks.sh updated: --selection-file <path> flag to use alternate selection file
configs/run_selected_tasks.sh: --use-case-category <A-J> filter within the selection file
New ccb_mcp_* suite directories recognized by aggregate_status.py and generate_manifest.py (add prefix mappings)
validate_tasks_preflight.py --all scans ccb_mcp_* directories
Smoke test: configs/run_selected_tasks.sh --selection-file configs/selected_mcp_unique_tasks.json --dry-run succeeds
userStories.notes: The selection file is separate from selected_benchmark_tasks.json to avoid bloating the existing file. The --selection-file flag is the bridge. Need to add ccb_mcp_* prefixes to DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE in generate_manifest.py, aggregate_status.py, and run_judge.py.

userStories.title: Report generator integration for retrieval metrics
userStories.description: Extend eval report to include MCP retrieval metrics sliced by suite, category, and repo-set.
acceptanceCriteria: generate_eval_report.py includes 'MCP Retrieval Performance' section when retrieval_metrics.json exists
Per-task: oracle_coverage, time_to_first_hit, repos/orgs touched
Per-suite aggregate: mean oracle_coverage, mean repos_touched
Baseline vs MCP-Full comparison table per task
Tool usage breakdown: which MCP tools drive discovery
Backwards-compatible: no retrieval data = no section
python3 scripts/generate_eval_report.py runs without errors
userStories.notes: The report should clearly show that baseline has lower oracle_coverage on mcp_only repos.

userStories.title: Rubric judge integration for hybrid tasks
userStories.description: Extend run_judge.py for hybrid evaluation: deterministic checks + rubric quality scoring.
acceptanceCriteria: run_judge.py auto-detects tests/criteria.json and includes it in judge evaluation
criteria.json format: [{metric, description (AAA), max_score}]
judge_result.json includes criteria_scores when criteria.json present
Hybrid scoring: composite = 0.6 * verifier_reward + 0.4 * rubric_score (configurable)
--hybrid flag enables hybrid mode
Backwards compatible for tasks without criteria.json
userStories.notes: Only E and J-family DS tasks need rubric judging initially. Most tasks are fully deterministic.

userStories.title: End-to-end validation run
userStories.description: Run full starter pack (12 tasks) through both configs, validate infrastructure, produce initial results for threshold calibration.
acceptanceCriteria: All 12 starter tasks pass validity gate
All 12 pass validate_tasks_preflight.py static checks
At least 8 tasks complete under sourcegraph_full (agent runs, eval returns score, result.json written)
At least 8 tasks under baseline produce lower oracle_coverage than SG_full
Retrieval metrics extracted for all completed tasks
Report generator produces MCP-unique section
No infra failures
Results in runs/staging/
Raw scores collected for threshold calibration in docs/MCP_UNIQUE_CALIBRATION.md
userStories.notes: Goal is validating plumbing and collecting calibration data. Document actual recall/precision scores so we can set thresholds in a follow-up.

userStories.title: Documentation and extensibility guide
userStories.description: Write docs covering the MCP-unique task system end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/MCP_UNIQUE_TASKS.md: Overview, Suite Structure (ccb_mcp_* mapping), Task Authoring (generator + oracle curator), Evaluation Framework, Running Tasks, Retrieval Metrics, Deep Search Tasks, Extending Categories
Architecture diagram (ASCII)
Worked example: authoring CCX-dep-trace-001 from scratch using generator + oracle curator
docs/EXTENSIBILITY.md updated with MCP-unique section
docs/CONFIGS.md updated with --selection-file and --use-case-category
docs/SCORING_SEMANTICS.md updated with oracle checks and hybrid scoring
CLAUDE.md updated
userStories.notes: Include the design decisions (Q1-Q10) as rationale. Reference PRDBench and SWE-Factory. Document the cross-host deferral and how to add it later.
